profile.name: Akobo Minerals AB
profile.about: Akobo Minerals AB (publ) is a Scandinavian-based gold producer and exploration company with operations in Ethiopia, listed on Euronext Growth Oslo and the Frankfurt Stock Exchange under ticker AKOBO. Founded in 2009 and incorporated in Sweden (Gothenburg), with operational HQ in Oslo, the company holds a mining licence (16 km²) and exploration licence (182 km²) in the Gambela region and Dima Woreda, southwest Ethiopia. Its Segele mine is one of the world's highest-grade gold deposits and commenced production in 2022. A second key target, Joru, covers a larger area with lower grade. The Q4 2025 was the company's strongest operational quarter with revenues of SEK 29.8M (USD 3.2M). CEO is Jørgen Evjen. Ethiopian Investment Holdings is a shareholder.

people.name: Jørgen Evjen
people.title: Chief Executive Officer
people.bio: CEO and co-founder. Based in Norway. Led the company from exploration through to gold production at Segele.

people.name: Johan Sjöberg
people.title: Chief Exploration Manager
people.bio: Based in Sweden. Leads geological exploration at the Segele and Joru projects.

keyFacts.label: Operations
keyFacts.value: Gambela region & Dima Woreda, southwest Ethiopia

keyFacts.label: Key deposit
keyFacts.value: Segele — one of world's highest-grade gold deposits
